edges.description: Activated PIK3CA signaling drives pathological vascular growth.
edges.source: Somatic PIK3CA activation in malformed tissue

edges.description: Activated PI3K/mTOR signaling promotes endothelial overgrowth and pathological sprouting.
Mosaic PI3K pathway activation also promotes regional tissue overgrowth.
Persistent endothelial dysregulation produces malformed superficial and venous vascular channels.
Capillary malformation burden is expressed clinically as nevus flammeus.
Venous malformation burden is expressed clinically as varicose veins.
Extensive vascular malformations contribute to localized pain.
Regional overgrowth is expressed clinically as limb hypertrophy.

edges.target: Somatic PIK3CA activation in malformed tissue

nodes.description: PIK3CA activation increases endothelial proliferation and angiogenic behavior, promoting malformed vascular channels.

evidence.reference_title: PI3K/mTOR inhibition promotes the regression of experimental vascular malformations driven by PIK3CA-activating mutations
evidence.snippet: Moreover, active forms of PIK3CA strongly promote the angiogenic sprouting.

nodes.description: Constitutive PIK3CA activation drives PI3K/mTOR pathway signaling in malformed endothelial tissue.

evidence.reference_title: PI3K/mTOR inhibition promotes the regression of experimental vascular malformations driven by PIK3CA-activating mutations
evidence.snippet: Our findings reveal that PIK3CA mutations have a key role in the pathogenesis of VM and PIK3CA-driven experimental lesions can be effectively treated by PI3K/mTOR inhibitors.

nodes.description: Persistent endothelial dysregulation produces the capillary and venous malformation burden that defines the vascular component of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Lymphatic and other vascular malformative/overgrowth disorders are caused by somatic mutations in PIK3CA.
evidence.snippet: KTS has been recognized for more than a century 23; we defined the condition as comprised of overgrowth, cutaneous capillary and lymphatic malformation, and persistence of embryonic vasculature in an extremity 12, 14.

nodes.description: Segmental overgrowth involving soft tissue and bone commonly affects the involved limb.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical treatment of varicose veins and venous malformations in Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All had varicose veins, 36 (73%) had limb hypertrophy, and 33 (67%) had capillary malformations, with two of three clinical features present in all.

nodes.description: Pain is a common symptomatic consequence of the extensive venous and vascular malformation burden.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical treatment of varicose veins and venous malformations in Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The most frequent symptom was pain (N = 43, 88%).

nodes.description: Cutaneous capillary malformation is one of the characteristic surface markers of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical treatment of varicose veins and venous malformations in Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All had varicose veins, 36 (73%) had limb hypertrophy, and 33 (67%) had capillary malformations, with two of three clinical features present in all.

evidence.reference_title: Lymphatic and other vascular malformative/overgrowth disorders are caused by somatic mutations in PIK3CA.
evidence.snippet: Somatic PIK3CA mutations are the most common cause of isolated LMs and disorders in which LM is a component feature.

nodes.description: Mosaic PIK3CA activation also drives regional soft-tissue and bony enlargement of the affected extremity.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical treatment of varicose veins and venous malformations in Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All had varicose veins, 36 (73%) had limb hypertrophy, and 33 (67%) had capillary malformations, with two of three clinical features present in all.

nodes.description: Activating mosaic PIK3CA mutations arise in affected tissue and initiate the vascular malformation and overgrowth phenotype.

nodes.description: Enlarged superficial veins are a defining feature of the syndrome and part of the classic Klippel-Trenaunay triad.

evidence.reference_title: Surgical treatment of varicose veins and venous malformations in Klippel-Trenaunay syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All had varicose veins, 36 (73%) had limb hypertrophy, and 33 (67%) had capillary malformations, with two of three clinical features present in all.
